text.title: Page Object Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that rot Page Object suites within 6-12 months. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Locator soup — page objects accumulate CSS, XPath, role-based, test-id, and text-based locators in arbitrary mixes, broken by any UI change.
failure-mode.detector: page object has more than 2 locator priorities used OR > 20% of locators are CSS / XPath without a justifying comment
failure-mode.repair: Run scripts/locator-audit.py and fix all CSS/XPath locators that have a role-based alternative. Add data-testid attributes in the UI repo for the rest. Stage this work into a one-time refactor PR; do not let it leak into feature PRs.

failure-mode.description: Implicit / time-based waits — page object methods include waitForTimeout, sleep, cy.wait(2000), making the suite simultaneously slow and flaky.
failure-mode.detector: grep -r "waitForTimeout\|cy.wait([0-9]\|setTimeout" pages/ tests/ returns any matches
failure-mode.repair: Replace every time-based wait with a condition-based wait: `await locator.waitFor({ state: 'visible' })` in Playwright, `cy.findByRole(...).should('be.visible')` in Cypress. Pre-commit hook rejects new time-based waits.

failure-mode.description: Fat page objects — single page object accumulates 30+ methods covering features that belong to sub-components, making the file unreadable and high-conflict in git.
failure-mode.detector: page object file > 400 lines OR > 25 public methods OR > 5 unrelated feature areas in one class
failure-mode.repair: Decompose into sub-components: dashboard.page.ts becomes dashboard.page.ts (top-level), dashboard-sidebar.component.ts, dashboard-table.component.ts. Each sub-component has its own contract test.

failure-mode.description: Test-helper sprawl — `tests/helpers/` accumulates ad-hoc utilities that should be page-object methods, defeating the encapsulation.
failure-mode.detector: tests/helpers/ directory has > 5 files OR helpers call page.locator / cy.get directly
failure-mode.repair: Audit each helper: if it touches DOM, move the logic into the relevant page object as a public method. tests/helpers/ should contain only pure utility code (date formatting, test-data builders) — no DOM interaction.

failure-mode.description: Contract test snapshot rot — page-object PR updates locators but contract-test snapshot is not regenerated, downstream tests break.
failure-mode.detector: contract_test.last_passed_at older than 30 days OR contract-test snapshot in git was last edited before the page-object file was last edited
failure-mode.repair: Pre-commit hook runs the contract test when the page-object file changes; updates the snapshot in the same PR. Reviewer must read the snapshot diff and confirm the locator change was intentional.

failure-mode.description: Implementation leak — test files reach into the DOM directly when the page object does not yet expose the needed action, accumulating duplicate selector logic.
failure-mode.detector: grep -r "page.locator\|cy.get" tests/ returns matches outside pages/ subdirectory
failure-mode.repair: For each direct-DOM-access in tests/, add the missing user-action method to the corresponding page object. Pre-commit lint flags any direct DOM access in tests/ from this point forward.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: An AI-generated test PR that imports the page object and then bypasses it with direct `page.locator` calls should be rejected at review. The page-object abstraction only pays off when every test honors it; one exception erodes the discipline.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: testing lead walks the page-object directory, looks at the locator-audit summary, picks the worst-offending file, and assigns a refactor task. Maintenance debt in E2E suites compounds; one quarterly cleanup keeps it bounded.
